CAT.rationale: CAT trades at a compressed 14.7x P/E near the low end of its one-year range despite strong margins and ROE, but the recent -8.4% three-month pullback and cyclical end-market risk temper the upside case.
key_factors: P/E of 14.7x sits near the bottom of its 13.0–21.1x one-year range, offering valuation cushion
ROE of 63.7% and net margin of 16.8% reflect best-in-class profitability for a cyclical industrial
Stock has pulled back -8.4% over the past 3 months from ~350 to 333, creating a better entry point after a +36.4% one-year run
Q1 2024 EPS of $5.75 is roughly flat vs Q1 2023's $5.27, suggesting earnings momentum is decelerating

AMZN.rationale: Strong revenue growth and margin expansion underpin a constructive view, but a stretched P/E of ~55x limits upside and tempers conviction.
key_factors: Q1 2024 revenue of $143.31B up from $143.08B prior-year quarter with EPS rising to $0.98 from $0.94, showing accelerating profitability
Operating margin improved to 8% with operating income of $15.31B, signaling AWS and cost discipline gains
P/E diluted at 54.7x is near the low end of its 1y range (50.0–326.2) but still elevated in absolute terms, capping risk-adjusted reward
Stock up +51.1% over the past year and +6.8% over 3 months, suggesting much of the recovery is already priced in

Analysts soured with multiple downgrades: 'No Quick Rebound' and 'This Doesn't End Well'
PE at 22.0x is at the low end of its 1y range (22.0–37.5x) but earnings trajectory is declining: EPS fell from 1.03 to 0.77 over last two quarters
